tasks.title: Design Article Firestore Schema
tasks.description: Create DB_SCHEMA.md documenting the Firestore schema for articles collection with all required fields including thumbnailURL, createdAt, and isUserArticle flag
tasks.details:
Create `backend/docs/DB_SCHEMA.md` with schema definition:

```
articles/{docId}
├── id: string (auto-generated)
├── author: string (required)
├── title: string (required)
├── description: string
├── url: string?
├── publishedAt: timestamp (required)
├── content: string (required)
├── thumbnailURL: string (Cloud Storage path: media/articles/{filename})
├── createdAt: timestamp (required)
└── isUserArticle: boolean (true for user uploads)
```
Include field types, descriptions, nullability, and validation rules. Reference Cloud Storage path format exactly as `media/articles/{uuid}.{ext}`
tasks.testStrategy: Verify file exists with all required fields documented. Manually validate schema matches ArticleEntity extension requirements. Check thumbnailURL path format references correct Storage folder structure.

tasks.testStrategy: Screenshot console showing Anonymous enabled. Test via curl or Firebase CLI: `firebase auth:export` shows anonymous provider active.

subtasks.title: Access Firebase Console Authentication Section
subtasks.description: Navigate to the Firebase Console and open the Authentication section for the symmetry-news project matching firebase_options.dart.
subtasks.details: Log into Firebase Console at console.firebase.google.com. Select the exact project ID from firebase_options.dart (symmetry-news-[unique]). Click 'Authentication' in left sidebar, then 'Sign-in method' tab.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify correct project selected by checking project ID in header matches firebase_options.dart. Confirm landing on Sign-in method page.

subtasks.title: Enable Anonymous Sign-in Provider
subtasks.description: Locate and enable the Anonymous authentication provider in the Sign-in methods configuration.
subtasks.details: Scroll to find 'Anonymous' provider in the list of sign-in methods. Click 'Edit' or pencil icon next to Anonymous. Toggle 'Enable' switch to ON position.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify Anonymous provider shows 'Enabled' status with green indicator before saving.

subtasks.title: Save Anonymous Authentication Configuration
subtasks.description: Apply the changes by saving the updated sign-in method configuration in Firebase Console.
subtasks.details: Click blue 'Save' button at bottom of Anonymous provider configuration dialog. Wait for success confirmation toast/message to appear.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm save success by seeing green confirmation message and Anonymous status remains 'Enabled' on Sign-in methods page.

subtasks.title: Capture Verification Screenshot
subtasks.description: Take screenshot of Sign-in methods page showing Anonymous provider enabled for documentation.
subtasks.details: Full page screenshot of Authentication > Sign-in method showing: Project name, Anonymous 'Enabled' status, timestamp visible. Save as 'task20_anonymous_enabled.png'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Screenshot clearly shows project ID matching firebase_options.dart and Anonymous status=Enabled.

subtasks.title: Verify Anonymous Provider Active via CLI
subtasks.description: Use Firebase CLI to confirm anonymous authentication provider is active in the project.
subtasks.details: Run `firebase auth:export --project=symmetry-news-[id] > auth_export.json`. Check JSON output contains anonymous provider configuration or test anonymous sign-in succeeds.
subtasks.testStrategy: `firebase auth:export` completes without errors. Verify anonymous provider listed or run test anonymous sign-in via Flutter app/emulator.

tasks.title: Implement Firebase Auth initialization in main.dart
tasks.description: Extend existing Firebase.initializeApp() to include auth state restoration and anonymous sign-in at app bootstrap (FR3)
tasks.details: In main.dart after Firebase.initializeApp(options: DefaultFirebaseOptions.currentPlatform): Check FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser. If null, call await FirebaseAuth.instance.signInAnonymously(). Use try-catch with debugPrint for errors. Call runApp only after auth ready.
tasks.testStrategy: Add debugPrint('Current user: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser?.uid}'); Verify non-null UID in logs after cold start before runApp executes.

subtasks.title: Verify and update main.dart Firebase initialization
subtasks.description: Ensure WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() and Firebase.initializeApp() are correctly set up before auth logic.
subtasks.details: Add WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() at start of main(). Confirm Firebase.initializeApp(options: DefaultFirebaseOptions.currentPlatform) exists and completes successfully before proceeding to auth checks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify no initialization errors in console logs after cold start.

subtasks.title: Implement current user check after Firebase init
subtasks.description: Check FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser after Firebase.initializeApp() completes.
subtasks.details: After await Firebase.initializeApp(), add: User? currentUser = FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser; Use debugPrint('Auth state restored: ${currentUser != null}'); to log restoration status.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check logs show 'Auth state restored: false' on first cold start.

subtasks.title: Add anonymous sign-in logic with null user check
subtasks.description: If currentUser is null, trigger anonymous authentication.
subtasks.details: Wrap in if (currentUser == null) { await FirebaseAuth.instance.signInAnonymously(); } Ensure this happens before runApp() to guarantee auth readiness at app bootstrap.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify signInAnonymously() called only when currentUser is null.

subtasks.title: Implement comprehensive try-catch error handling
subtasks.description: Wrap auth initialization and sign-in in try-catch with debug logging.
subtasks.details: Use try { ... Firebase.initializeApp() ... currentUser check ... signInAnonymously() ... } catch (e) { debugPrint('Auth init error: $e'); } rethrow or handle gracefully before runApp().
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate network error, verify error logged but app still launches.

subtasks.title: Add auth readiness verification and call runApp
subtasks.description: Verify final auth state and launch app only after auth is ready.
subtasks.details: After auth logic: debugPrint('Current user: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser?.uid}'); runApp(MyApp()); Ensure UID is non-null in logs before runApp executes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm non-null UID printed in logs after cold start before runApp, verifying complete auth flow.

tasks.title: Create Auth readiness stream wrapper
tasks.description: Wrap app with StreamBuilder on authStateChanges() to block UI until authenticated (FR3, FR4)
tasks.details: Create AuthGate widget using StreamBuilder<User?>(stream: FirebaseAuth.instance.authStateChanges()). Show loading spinner if !snapshot.hasData, else show MaterialApp home. Handle sign-in errors with SnackBar in debug mode.
tasks.testStrategy: Cold start app, verify StreamBuilder waits for auth completion. Logs show auth state transitions. Debugger confirms currentUser non-null before upload UI accessible.

subtasks.title: Create AuthGate widget skeleton
subtasks.description: Create the basic AuthGate StatelessWidget structure with StreamBuilder<User?> listening to FirebaseAuth.instance.authStateChanges()
subtasks.details: Create file lib/presentation/widgets/auth_gate.dart. Import firebase_auth and flutter/material. Define class AuthGate extends StatelessWidget with StreamBuilder<User?>(stream: FirebaseAuth.instance.authStateChanges()).
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify widget compiles without errors and StreamBuilder is properly initialized with authStateChanges stream.

subtasks.title: Implement loading spinner state
subtasks.description: Handle !snapshot.hasData case by showing centered CircularProgressIndicator
subtasks.details: In StreamBuilder builder, add if (!snapshot.hasData) return Scaffold(body: Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator())). Ensure full-screen loading blocks UI access.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cold start app shows loading spinner until auth state resolves. Verify spinner is centered and responsive.

subtasks.title: Implement authenticated state with MaterialApp
subtasks.description: When snapshot.hasData, return complete MaterialApp with home set to main app content
subtasks.details: In else clause of StreamBuilder, return MaterialApp(home: MainAppContent() or existing home widget). Ensure proper theme and routing setup. Import main app widget.
subtasks.testStrategy: After auth completes, verify MaterialApp renders with correct home screen. Check no UI flicker during transition.

subtasks.title: Add debug mode error handling with SnackBar
subtasks.description: Handle snapshot.error by showing SnackBar with error message only in debug mode using kDebugMode
subtasks.details: Import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart' show kDebugMode. Add else if (snapshot.hasError && kDebugMode) {ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(SnackBar(content: Text(snapshot.error.toString()))); return LoadingSpinner();}
subtasks.testStrategy: Force auth error in debug mode, verify SnackBar shows error message. Confirm no SnackBar in release mode.

subtasks.title: Integrate AuthGate as app root widget
subtasks.description: Replace main.dart runApp with AuthGate and test full auth flow
subtasks.details: In main.dart, change runApp(MyApp()) to runApp(AuthGate()). Ensure Firebase is initialized before runApp. Test cold start to authenticated state transition.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cold start app: verify waits for auth completion, logs show auth state transitions, currentUser non-null before main UI accessible.

tasks.title: Add auth error handling and logging
tasks.description: Implement visible failure handling for sign-in errors (provider disabled, network issues) (FR3)
tasks.details: In signInAnonymously() try-catch: catch FirebaseAuthException with codes 'operation-not-allowed', 'network-request-failed'. debugPrint full stack trace. Optional: SnackBar('Auth failed: ${e.message}').
tasks.testStrategy: Disable Anonymous in console, restart app, verify error logs appear and app doesn't crash. Re-enable, confirm recovery.

subtasks.title: Add try-catch block to signInAnonymously method
subtasks.description: Wrap the FirebaseAuth.instance.signInAnonymously() call in a try-catch block to handle FirebaseAuthException errors.
subtasks.details: Locate the signInAnonymously() method in the authentication service or cubit. Add try { await FirebaseAuth.instance.signInAnonymously(); } on FirebaseAuthException catch (e) { ... } ensuring the catch block is implemented next.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify no syntax errors after adding try-catch structure. Ensure successful sign-in path still works without throwing unhandled exceptions.

subtasks.title: Implement specific error code checking
subtasks.description: Check for 'operation-not-allowed' and 'network-request-failed' error codes in the catch block.
subtasks.details: In the catch block, add if (e.code == 'operation-not-allowed' || e.code == 'network-request-failed') { ... } to specifically handle provider disabled and network issues as specified in FR3.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test verifies correct error code detection for both specified codes. Test other error codes fall through to default handling.

subtasks.title: Add debugPrint for full stack trace logging
subtasks.description: Log the full error details including stack trace using debugPrint for debugging purposes.
subtasks.details: Inside the specific error handling block, add debugPrint('Auth error: ${e.code}'); debugPrint('Message: ${e.message}'); debugPrintStack(stackTrace: e.stackTrace); to capture complete error information.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run app in debug mode, trigger errors, verify debug console shows full stack trace and error codes. Check logs are formatted correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement optional SnackBar user feedback
subtasks.description: Add user-visible error feedback using SnackBar to show authentication failure message.
subtasks.details: Use ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(SnackBar(content: Text('Auth failed: ${e.message}'), backgroundColor: Colors.red)); Ensure context is available or use a state management solution to trigger SnackBar.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify SnackBar appears with correct error message for both error codes. Test SnackBar styling and auto-dismiss behavior.

subtasks.title: Add general error fallback and testing setup
subtasks.description: Handle unexpected errors and prepare test scenarios for verification.
subtasks.details: Add else clause for other FirebaseAuthException codes with generic logging. Document test steps: disable anonymous auth in Firebase console, restart app, verify error handling, re-enable and test recovery.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: Disable anonymous auth in Firebase console, attempt sign-in, verify error SnackBar appears, logs show correct codes, app doesn't crash. Re-enable auth and verify normal flow works.

tasks.title: Verify upload flow waits for auth completion
tasks.description: Ensure article/thumbnail upload routes only accessible after FirebaseAuth.currentUser != null (FR4)
tasks.details: Guard upload button/form with if(FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser == null) return Future.value(); before Storage/Firestore calls. Add auth check in upload service methods.
tasks.testStrategy: Add print('Auth check: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser != null}'); before upload. Verify true before any write operations in successful flow.

subtasks.title: Add auth guard to upload button and form handlers
subtasks.description: Implement FirebaseAuth check in UI upload button onPressed and form submission handlers to prevent initiation if not authenticated
subtasks.details: In upload screen/widget, wrap onPressed/onSubmit with: if(FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser == null) { showSnackBar('Please sign in first'); return; } before calling upload service. Add print('UI Auth check: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser != null}');
subtasks.testStrategy: Tap upload button while signed out - verify no service call, snackbar shows. Sign in then tap - verify print shows true and proceeds.

subtasks.title: Implement auth check in upload service entry methods
subtasks.description: Add authentication guard at the start of main uploadArticle and uploadArticleThumbnail service methods
subtasks.details: In UploadService class, add to uploadArticle/uploadArticleThumbnail: if(FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser == null) return Future.value(Left(Failure('User not authenticated'))); Add print('Service Auth check: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser != null}'); before Storage/Firestore calls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Call service methods while signed out - verify returns Failure immediately, print shows false. Sign in then call - verify print shows true.

subtasks.title: Add auth state stream listener for reactive UI protection
subtasks.description: Subscribe to FirebaseAuth.authStateChanges() stream to disable upload UI elements when auth state changes to null
subtasks.details: In upload screen initState or provider, listen to FirebaseAuth.instance.authStateChanges(). Listen for null user to set isUploadEnabled=false and show loading/redirect. Dispose listener properly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to upload screen signed out - verify upload disabled. Sign in - verify enables. Sign out during upload screen - verify immediately disables.

subtasks.title: Verify auth completion before Storage and Firestore operations
subtasks.description: Ensure all file upload and document write operations wait for currentUser to be fully loaded and non-null
subtasks.details: Before every Storage.uploadFile and Firestore.add/set, add await Future.delayed(Duration(milliseconds: 100)); then recheck FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser != null. Use User?.getIdToken() to force refresh if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Monitor logs during fresh app start + quick upload attempt - verify all auth checks pass true before any write operations execute.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive testing and documentation
subtasks.description: Create test cases for auth-blocked uploads and document the auth guard implementation pattern
subtasks.details: Write widget tests simulating signed out/in states calling upload flows. Add README section: 'Upload Auth Guards' explaining the 3-layer protection (UI/Service/Operation). Update Task 24 implementation details.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full test suite: verify 100% auth check coverage, no uploads succeed without auth, all failure paths return user-friendly errors.

tasks.title: Fix thumbnailURL requirement for Firestore rules
tasks.description: Ensure Firestore writes include non-empty thumbnailURL to satisfy existing rules (FR5)
tasks.details: In article submission: if no thumbnail selected, set thumbnailURL: 'https://via.placeholder.com/300x200/cccccc/666666?text=No+Image'. Require thumbnail before submit OR use placeholder. Validate before Firestore write.
tasks.testStrategy: Submit article without thumbnail using placeholder. Verify Firestore write succeeds (no permission-denied on thumbnailURL). Check rules simulator.

subtasks.title: Update ArticleModel toFirestore with thumbnailURL fallback
subtasks.description: Modify ArticleModel.toFirestore() to set placeholder URL if thumbnailURL is null or empty
subtasks.details: In ArticleModel.toFirestore(), add logic: 'thumbnailURL': thumbnailURL?.isNotEmpty == true ? thumbnailURL : 'https://via.placeholder.com/300x200/cccccc/666666?text=No+Image'
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: create ArticleModel with null/empty thumbnailURL, verify toFirestore() returns placeholder URL

subtasks.title: Add client-side thumbnailURL validation before upload
subtasks.description: Create validation function in article submission service to ensure thumbnailURL is never empty before Firestore write
subtasks.details: Add validateArticleSubmission() that checks article.thumbnailURL != null && article.thumbnailURL.isNotEmpty, throws exception if invalid
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test validation with null/empty thumbnailURL cases, verify throws ValidationException

subtasks.title: Update FirestoreArticleDataSource uploadArticle method
subtasks.description: Modify uploadArticle to call validation and use updated ArticleModel serialization
subtasks.details: In FirestoreArticleDataSourceImpl.uploadArticle(): validateArticleSubmission(article); await firestore.collection('articles').add(article.toFirestore());
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: call uploadArticle with valid article (placeholder thumbnail), verify Firestore write succeeds

subtasks.title: Update article submission UI to handle thumbnail fallback
subtasks.description: Modify submission form to automatically use placeholder if no thumbnail selected, disable submit until valid
subtasks.details: In submission screen: use ValueNotifier for thumbnail state, set placeholder URL automatically if null, enable submit button only after validation passes
subtasks.testStrategy: Widget test: submit form without thumbnail selection, verify placeholder URL used and submission succeeds

subtasks.title: Test Firestore rules compliance and rules simulator
subtasks.description: Verify writes succeed with placeholder thumbnailURL using Firebase console rules simulator and integration tests
subtasks.details: Run integration test with Firebase emulator, test Firestore rules simulator with article containing placeholder thumbnailURL, confirm no permission denied errors
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: complete article submission flow without thumbnail, verify document created in Firestore with valid thumbnailURL field

tasks.title: End-to-end test auth-gated uploads
tasks.description: Verify complete flow: cold start → auth → upload succeeds against deployed rules (Acceptance Criteria)
tasks.details: Deploy firestore.rules/storage.rules to same project. Run app, fill article form + thumbnail, submit. Verify Firestore document created and Storage file uploaded.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: flutter test with Firebase emulators OR device test. Confirm no permission-denied errors. Document successful paths.

subtasks.title: Deploy Firestore and Storage security rules
subtasks.description: Deploy the latest firestore.rules and storage.rules to the target Firebase project to enforce auth-gated access.
subtasks.details: Run `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules,storage` from the firebase directory. Verify deployment success in Firebase console Rules tab. Ensure rules require authentication for writes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check Firebase console Rules tab shows deployed rules with correct auth requirements.

subtasks.title: Start app from cold state and complete authentication
subtasks.description: Launch the Flutter app from cold start, complete full authentication flow until FirebaseAuth.currentUser != null.
subtasks.details: Run `flutter run` (device or emulator). Navigate through any splash/onboarding screens. Sign in with valid email/password or social provider. Add debug print: print('Auth state: ${FirebaseAuth.instance.currentUser != null}');
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify auth stream fires sign-in event and currentUser is non-null before proceeding.

subtasks.title: Navigate to article upload screen and fill form
subtasks.description: Access the article upload screen, complete all form fields and select thumbnail image.
subtasks.details: Tap FAB or navigate to upload screen. Fill title, author, content, publishedAt date. Pick thumbnail from gallery. Verify form validation passes and preview shows image.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm no auth guards block access and form shows all fields enabled.

subtasks.title: Submit upload and monitor progress
subtasks.description: Trigger article submission and observe the complete upload flow through loading to success state.
subtasks.details: Tap submit button. Watch ArticleUploadCubit states: Initial → Loading → Success. Note any console logs or SnackBar messages. Ensure no permission-denied errors appear.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify UI shows loading spinner, submit button disabled during upload, success navigation occurs.

subtasks.title: Verify Firestore document and Storage file creation
subtasks.description: Confirm successful creation of Firestore article document and Storage thumbnail file against deployed rules.
subtasks.details: Check Firebase console: Firestore → articles collection for new document with correct fields/thumbnailURL. Storage → thumbnails/ for uploaded file. Verify document matches form data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Screenshot console views. Test unauth access blocked by attempting read without login. Document entire successful path.

tasks.title: Add auth verification negative test
tasks.description: Confirm app fails gracefully when auth unavailable (Acceptance Criteria verification step)
tasks.details: Temporary test: disable Anonymous provider. Run app, attempt upload → verify sign-in error surfaces (logs/UI). Re-enable provider.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual test: toggle provider on/off in console. Verify error handling works, app doesn't allow unauthenticated writes.

subtasks.title: Navigate to Firebase Auth Console
subtasks.description: Access Firebase project console and go to Authentication section to prepare for provider modification.
subtasks.details: Open browser, login to Firebase console, select symmetry-news-[id] project, navigate to Authentication > Sign-in method tab where providers are managed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify correct project selected and Authentication section loads without errors.

subtasks.title: Disable Anonymous Authentication Provider
subtasks.description: Temporarily disable the Anonymous sign-in provider to simulate auth unavailable scenario.
subtasks.details: In Sign-in method tab, find Anonymous provider, click pencil/edit icon, toggle 'Enable' to OFF, click Save. Note any warnings about existing users.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm provider status shows DISABLED in console UI after saving changes.

subtasks.title: Run Flutter App and Trigger Upload
subtasks.description: Start the app and attempt to perform an article upload action to test unauthenticated behavior.
subtasks.details: Run `flutter run` from project root. Navigate to upload/create article screen. Fill required fields (title, content, etc.) and tap upload/submit button.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify app launches successfully but upload attempt triggers auth-related failure.

subtasks.title: Verify Sign-in Error Surfaces in Logs/UI
subtasks.description: Check that app handles missing auth gracefully by displaying sign-in error in UI and/or logs.
subtasks.details: Monitor Flutter console logs for FirebaseAuth errors. Check app UI for error dialogs, snackbars, or sign-in prompts. Expected: 'sign-in required', permission denied, or similar.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm error message clearly indicates authentication failure, no crashes occur, Firestore write blocked as per security rules.

subtasks.title: Re-enable Anonymous Provider and Cleanup
subtasks.description: Restore Anonymous provider to enabled state and verify normal app functionality returns.
subtasks.details: Return to Firebase console > Authentication > Sign-in method. Re-enable Anonymous provider, save changes. Restart app (`flutter run`), test successful anonymous upload.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify provider re-enabled, app allows anonymous sign-in and successful article upload with all fields validated.

tasks.title: Document Firebase Auth implementation

tasks.details: Append paragraph to docs/REPORT.md or README.md: 'App uses anonymous Firebase Authentication at startup via signInAnonymously() to satisfy Firestore/Storage rules requiring request.auth != null. currentUser ensured non-null before uploads.'
tasks.testStrategy: Verify documentation exists and accurately describes implementation. Include code snippets showing auth bootstrap.

subtasks.title: Locate target documentation file
subtasks.description: Identify whether to append content to docs/REPORT.md or README.md based on project documentation structure.
subtasks.details: Check project root for existing README.md and docs/REPORT.md files. Review current content to determine most appropriate location for Firebase Auth explanation. Ensure file exists or create if missing.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify target file exists and is accessible for editing.

subtasks.title: Draft Firebase anonymous auth explanation paragraph
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive paragraph explaining anonymous auth usage for security rules compliance.
subtasks.details: Use provided text as base: 'App uses anonymous Firebase Authentication at startup via signInAnonymously() to satisfy Firestore/Storage rules requiring request.auth != null. currentUser ensured non-null before uploads.' Expand with context from security rules (Task 3).
subtasks.testStrategy: Review paragraph for technical accuracy against Firebase Auth docs and project security rules.

subtasks.title: Add relevant code snippet to documentation
subtasks.description: Include actual code snippet showing signInAnonymously() implementation and currentUser null-check.
subtasks.details: Extract or recreate the auth bootstrap code from implementation (likely Tasks 21/22/26). Format as Markdown code block with Dart syntax highlighting. Reference exact file locations and line numbers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify code snippet matches actual implementation and demonstrates auth flow correctly.

subtasks.title: Append documentation to target file
subtasks.description: Insert the drafted paragraph and code snippet into the chosen documentation file.
subtasks.details: Add content under appropriate section heading like '## Firebase Authentication' or '### Security Implementation'. Use Markdown formatting: H3 for subsection, paragraph text, code block. Ensure logical flow with existing content.

subtasks.title: Verify documentation accuracy and completeness
subtasks.description: Review final documentation against acceptance criteria and test strategy requirements.
